role: Former Minister of Justice (2013–2017); former Minister of Interior (2005–2008)
bio: Mostafa Pourmohammadi is a cleric who served as Interior Minister under [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad) (2005–2008) and later as Justice Minister under [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani) (2013–2017). His appointment by Rouhani was controversial and drew protests from human rights organisations, as Pourmohammadi had served as a member of the 'Death Committee' — the three-person panel in Tehran that approved the mass execution of thousands of political prisoners in 1988 following [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s fatwa. He later explicitly defended the executions. He ran in the 2024 presidential election.
editorial_notes: Born 1960. Member of 1988 Death Committee in Tehran. Interior Minister 2005–2008. Justice Minister 2013–2017. Ran in 2024 presidential election. Defended 1988 executions publicly.
